title: rubus lugdunensis
intro: Rubus lugdunensis, also known as the Lyon raspberry or Lyon bramble, is a species of raspberry native to the Lyon region of France and parts of Eastern Europe. It is a perennial shrub with edible fruits, rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. Rubus lugdunensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
